chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P. No.D- 3021 of 2018 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case. 27.10.2020 Mr. Ahmed Nawaz Chang, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional Advocate General, Sindh alongwith DSP Mumtaz Ali (I.O of Crime No.11 of 2019) and SIP Tasleem Ahmed Bhurgri SHO P.S Butho Forest. Mr. Khadim Hussain Soomro, Advocate for respondents No.5 and 6. = DSP Mumtaz Ali (I.O of Crime No.11 of 2019) is present and files report to the effect that upon the direction of this Court contained under order dated 28.02.2019, an F.I.R. bearing Crime No.11 of 2019, under section 364 PPC was registered at Police Station Butho Forest and nominated accused namely Abdul Shakoor, who confessed his guilt before the police; however, later on he has resiled from his statement before the Court concerned; therefore, interim challan of the case was submitted before the Court on 28.12.2019. It is also mentioned in the report that after completing necessary formalities, final challan of the case has also been submitted on 08.01.2020. He has further submitted in his said report that in view of the confession of accused Abdul Shakoor, section 364 PPC was converted into sections 302 and 201 PPC and the case is pending trial before the Court of MCTC Matiari vide Sessions Case No.47/2019 (re: The State v. Abdul Shakoor and others), where charge has also been framed against said accused. Per statement of learned counsel for respondents No.5 and 6, now the case is fixed before the trial Court for recording the evidence of prosecution witnesses on 07.12.2020.

Abdul Shakoor and others), where charge has also been framed against said accused. Per statement of learned counsel for respondents No.5 and 6, now the case is fixed before the trial Court for recording the evidence of prosecution witnesses on 07.12.2020. The report furnished by the I.O is taken on record and its copy has been provided to learned counsel for petitioner, who after going through the same has shown his satisfaction and submits that purpose of instant petition has been served, hence the same be disposed of. Other side has no objection. Order accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE S
